current_external_evidence: verifyUser() rejects missing bearer tokens and validates Supabase user identity
target_design: Require bearer auth for all /api/v1/ai-chat management and run endpoints
notes: bridge already gates management calls server-side

current_external_evidence: current source enforces auth but not explicit role tiers beyond user ownership
target_design: Add role-scoped checks on conversation/run attachment mutations
notes: backend must keep ownership checks even if RBAC remains coarse

current_external_evidence: no dedicated rate limiter in bridge source
target_design: Per-user/per-route limiter on run creation, attachment minting and streaming start
notes: current target backend rate limiter can be reused conceptually

current_external_evidence: no audit log emission observed in bridge source
target_design: Audit conversation create/update/delete, run create/cancel, attachment mint, artifact access
notes: must exist before any durable mutation is considered complete

current_external_evidence: provider keys and Hermes API calls are in backend code paths only
target_design: Frontend consumes only /api/v1 and never receives provider secrets
notes: match the existing Apoema boundary

current_external_evidence: HERMES_API_KEY and artifact keys live in server env only
target_design: Keep provider secrets in backend env / credential store
notes: no VITE_* exposure

current_external_evidence: state repository keys by user_id + chat_session_id
target_design: Conversation lookups must verify owner before detail or mutation
notes: must remain true if adapter is implemented later

current_external_evidence: run store saves user_id and run queries filter by owner
target_design: Run detail, cancel and event replay must verify owner
notes: important for SSE replay and cancel

current_external_evidence: attachment access validates userId/sessionId prefix
target_design: Artifact reads/mints must verify owner and session binding
notes: needs final artifact server contract implementation

current_external_evidence: max count, MIME allowlist and size caps exist in attachments.js
target_design: Only allow explicit types/sizes and block raw storage exposure
notes: policy is good enough for contract docs, not for durable storage yet

current_external_evidence: streaming events are terminal-aware but no explicit cancel route evidence
target_design: POST /runs/{run_id}/cancel must cancel upstream Hermes execution and stop SSE
notes: reserved target contract term

current_external_evidence: poll loop exists, but explicit timeout is not visible in inspected source
target_design: Bound run duration and SSE idle time with server timeout and fail-safe cancellation
notes: required before public streaming rollout

current_external_evidence: server injects status text while Hermes keeps running
target_design: Emit heartbeat events during long-running runs and while replaying SSE
notes: current status loop is the conceptual heartbeat

current_external_evidence: no explicit backpressure strategy observed
target_design: Throttle event fan-out and bounded replay windows
notes: needed for large outputs

current_external_evidence: error codes are sanitized and truncated in event parsing
target_design: Strip prompt content, secrets and raw upstream provider payloads from user-facing errors
notes: the bridge already shortens error excerpts

current_external_evidence: no prompt-injection note mechanism is visible
target_design: Mark attachment-originated text as untrusted and keep model/system prompts explicit
notes: must be documented in the adapter contract

current_external_evidence: tool events are normalized but no allowlist enforcement is visible
target_design: Allow only approved tool names in target adapter contract
notes: required for safer Hermes integration

current_external_evidence: poll interval is explicit but timeout boundary not evidenced
target_design: Hard timeout the upstream provider request and fail gracefully
notes: needs explicit timeout config in target

current_external_evidence: readJsonBody caps payloads at 1_000_000 bytes
target_design: Reject oversized run/attachment requests with 413
notes: current bridge already has a request cap

current_external_evidence: per-user state repository and Hermes session binding exist
target_design: Isolate conversation/run state per user and per session id
notes: adapter must preserve ownership and run scopes
